description: Rust-backed drop-in replacement for Yelp/detect-secrets with PyO3 bindings, targeting 10-50x speedup

userStories.title: Set up Rust workspace with PyO3 and maturin
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolding so I can start implementing the Rust core.
acceptanceCriteria: Cargo.toml workspace with two crates: crates/engine (pure Rust) and crates/python (PyO3 bindings)
pyproject.toml with maturin build backend targeting detect_secrets_rs package
crates/engine/src/lib.rs exists with a placeholder pub fn
crates/python/src/lib.rs exists with a #[pymodule] that exposes a version() function
maturin develop builds successfully
import detect_secrets_rs; detect_secrets_rs.version() works from Python
cargo test passes (even if only a placeholder test)
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement PotentialSecret data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core secret data structure to store and compare scan results.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust struct PotentialSecret with fields: secret_type, filename, secret_hash (SHA1), line_number, is_secret, is_verified
hash_secret(secret: &str) -> String using SHA1 hex digest
secret_value kept in memory but excluded from serialization
Equality based on (filename, secret_hash, secret_type) matching Python's fields_to_compare
Implements Hash + Eq for use in HashSet
JSON serialization matching Python's PotentialSecret.json() output format
load_secret_from_dict() deserialization from baseline JSON dict
PyO3 class with all methods exposed to Python
Unit tests for hashing, equality, and JSON round-trip
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement Shannon entropy calculation
userStories.description: As a user scanning files, I want entropy detection to be fast so that large repos scan in seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_shannon_entropy(data: &str, charset: &str) -> f64 in crates/engine
Exposed to Python via PyO3
Produces identical results to Python's HighEntropyStringsPlugin.calculate_shannon_entropy()
HexHighEntropyString numeric-only reduction path (int(data) branch) reimplemented
Unit tests comparing output against known Python values for Base64 and Hex charsets
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement regex-based plugin framework traits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Rust trait system for plugins so all built-in plugins can be ported.
acceptanceCriteria: trait SecretDetector with fn secret_type() -> &str and fn analyze_string(input: &str) -> Vec<PotentialSecret>
trait RegexBasedDetector: SecretDetector with fn denylist() -> &[Regex]
Default analyze_string implementation iterates denylist and collects matches
RegexBasedDetector handles tuple captures (named groups) like Python originals
build_assignment_regex() static helper ported from Python
PyO3 wrapper module that can expose any plugin as a Python class matching BasePlugin interface
At least one trivial test plugin implemented to validate the framework
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Port high-entropy string plugins
userStories.description: As a user, I want entropy-based secret detection so high-entropy strings are flagged.
acceptanceCriteria: Base64HighEntropyString plugin: charset, threshold, quoted-string regex, eager search mode
HexHighEntropyString plugin: charset, threshold, numeric-only reduction path
Both plugins implement SecretDetector trait
Both plugins exposed to Python via PyO3 matching original class interface
Unit tests verifying identical matches to Python originals for known test inputs
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Port regex plugins - cloud and infrastructure detectors
userStories.description: As a user, I want cloud provider secret detection so AWS/Azure/IBM keys are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: AWSKeyDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
AzureStorageKeyDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
ArtifactoryDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
CloudantDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
IBMCloudIAM implemented with denylist matching Python original
IBMCosHmac implemented with denylist matching Python original
SoftLayerDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
All implement SecretDetector trait and are exposed via PyO3
Unit tests for each plugin with known match/miss inputs
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Port regex plugins - auth and token detectors
userStories.description: As a user, I want auth token detection so leaked credentials are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: BasicAuthDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
DiscordDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
GitHubTokenDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
GitLabTokenDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
JwtTokenDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
PrivateKeyDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original
All implement SecretDetector trait and are exposed via PyO3
Unit tests for each plugin with known match/miss inputs
Build/lint passes

acceptanceCriteria: MailchimpDetector implemented with denylist matching Python original

userStories.title: Port KeywordDetector plugin
userStories.description: As a user, I want keyword-based secret detection with file-type-aware regex selection.
acceptanceCriteria: KeywordDetector implemented with FOLLOWED_BY_XXX regex patterns matching Python original
REGEX_BY_FILETYPE mapping ported (different regexes for .cls, .java, .py, .go, etc.)
DENYLIST keyword list matches Python original exactly
analyze_line() method uses correct regex based on filename extension
Plugin implements SecretDetector trait and is exposed via PyO3
Unit tests for multiple file types confirming identical matches to Python
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement heuristic and allowlist filters
userStories.description: As a user with filtering rules, I need heuristic filters to reduce false positives.
acceptanceCriteria: All heuristic filters ported: is_sequential_string, is_potential_uuid, is_likely_id_string, is_templated_secret, is_prefixed_with_dollar_sign, is_indirect_reference, is_lock_file, is_not_alphanumeric_string, is_swagger_file
Allowlist filter: is_line_allowlisted with pragma comment detection
is_invalid_file and is_non_text_file default filters
Each filter function has matching signature to Python original
All filters exposed via PyO3
Unit tests for each filter with known inputs
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement regex and wordlist filters
userStories.description: As a user, I need regex-based and wordlist-based filtering for custom exclusion rules.
acceptanceCriteria: should_exclude_line(line, regex) filter implemented
should_exclude_file(filename, regex) filter implemented
should_exclude_secret(secret, regex) filter implemented
Wordlist filter: should_exclude_secret with file loading from wordlist path
Filter dependency injection: filters declare which parameters they accept (filename, line, secret, context, plugin)
get_filters_with_parameter(*params) returns filters matching minimum parameter set
All filters exposed via PyO3
Unit tests for regex and wordlist filters
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement Settings and configuration system
userStories.description: As a user, I need my existing .secrets.baseline configuration to work unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings struct with plugins_used dict and filters_used dict
configure_plugins(config) accepts [{name: str, **kwargs}] format
configure_filters(config) accepts [{path: str, **kwargs}] format
default_settings() context manager enables all plugins + default filters
transient_settings(config) context manager for temporary override
get_settings() singleton with cache busting
configure_settings_from_baseline(baseline_dict) loads from baseline JSON
Settings JSON output matches Python format exactly
PyO3 class exposed to Python
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement SecretsCollection with merge and trim
userStories.description: As a user managing baselines, I need collection operations to update my baseline files.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust struct SecretsCollection with data: HashMap<String, HashSet<PotentialSecret>>
scan_file(filename) method that runs all plugins and filters on a file
merge(old_results) preserves is_secret/is_verified from old, updates with new
trim(scanned_results, filelist) performs set intersection + stale entry removal
json() produces output identical to Python's SecretsCollection.json()
load_from_baseline(baseline_dict) reconstructs from JSON
Iterator yields (filename, secret) tuples sorted by line number
PyO3 class with __eq__, __sub__, __len__, __bool__ operators matching Python
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement file scanning pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scan files for secrets with full plugin and filter application.
acceptanceCriteria: scan_file(filename) -> Vec<PotentialSecret> full pipeline: read, iterate lines, apply plugins, apply filters
scan_line(line) -> Vec<PotentialSecret> for adhoc string scanning
scan_diff(diff) -> Vec<PotentialSecret> for unified diff parsing
get_files_to_scan(paths, should_scan_all_files, root) with git-aware file discovery
Line-level filter application before plugin execution
Context-level filter application after plugin match
PyO3 wrappers for all scan functions
Integration test: scan a test file and verify results match Python output
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement baseline management
userStories.description: As a user, I need to create, load, and update baseline files in the existing format.
acceptanceCriteria: baseline::create(paths, should_scan_all_files, root, num_threads) -> SecretsCollection
baseline::load(baseline_dict, filename) -> SecretsCollection
baseline::load_from_file(filename) -> Dict parses JSON baseline
baseline::format_for_output(secrets, is_slim_mode) -> Dict with version, plugins_used, filters_used, results, generated_at
baseline::save_to_file(secrets, filename) writes JSON with 2-space indent
baseline::upgrade(baseline_dict) handles older baseline versions
Output JSON key ordering and formatting matches Python detect-secrets v1.5.0
Round-trip test: create baseline, save, load, compare
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement parallel file scanning with rayon
userStories.description: As a user scanning large repos, I want multi-threaded scanning faster than Python multiprocessing.
acceptanceCriteria: scan_files() uses rayon thread pool instead of Python multiprocessing
Thread count configurable with default num_cpus
Settings propagated to threads via Arc<Settings> without serialization
No GIL contention during Rust-side scanning
SecretsCollection.scan_files(filenames, num_threads) wired up to rayon
Unit test: parallel scan of multiple files produces same results as sequential
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Support Python custom plugins via PyO3 callback
userStories.description: As a user with custom plugins, I need them to work alongside Rust built-in plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom plugin loading from Python import path (e.g. my_module.MyPlugin)
Custom plugin loading from file path (e.g. file://path/to/plugin.py::MyPlugin)
Python plugins called via PyO3 when scanning: analyze_string(), analyze_line()
Custom Python filter loading via import path or file:// URI
get_mapping_from_secret_type_to_class() includes both Rust and Python plugins
Integration test with a dummy Python custom plugin
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI - scan and hook commands
userStories.description: As a user, I need detect-secrets scan and pre-commit hook so my CI/CD pipelines work.
acceptanceCriteria: detect-secrets scan command creates baseline from filesystem scan
detect-secrets-hook pre-commit hook entry point

CLI flags supported: --all-files, --baseline, --list-all-plugins, --no-verify, --only-verified, --exclude-lines, --exclude-files, --exclude-secrets, --word-list, --filter, --disable-filter, --disable-plugin, --slim
Exit codes match Python version behavior
Entry points registered in pyproject.toml
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Implement CLI - audit command
userStories.description: As a user, I need the audit command for interactive baseline review.
acceptanceCriteria: detect-secrets audit command launches interactive audit of baseline file
Displays secrets one at a time with file context
Supports marking as true/false positive
Supports --report flag for audit summary
Supports --diff flag for comparing two baselines
Exit codes match Python version behavior
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Port and pass existing test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need confidence that the Rust implementation behaves identically to Python.
acceptanceCriteria: All tests from detect-secrets/tests/ adapted for detect_secrets_rs package
Plugin tests: each plugin produces same matches as Python for same inputs
Baseline tests: round-trip compatibility verified
Filter tests: same filtering behavior for all built-in filters
Integration tests: end-to-end scan of test_data directory produces same results
All tests pass in CI
Build/lint passes

userStories.title: Run benchmark suite against Rust implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify performance improvements against the Python baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 benchmark modules in benchmark/ run against Rust implementation with zero code changes
Benchmark JSON output saved alongside Python baseline for comparison
Entropy microbenchmark: >10x faster than Python baseline
Regex microbenchmark: >5x faster than Python baseline
File scan mid-benchmark: >10x faster
Repo scan macro-benchmark: >10x faster multi-core
Baseline I/O mid-benchmark: >5x faster at large scale
Build/lint passes
userStories.notes: All 5 benchmarks run via compat shim with zero code changes. Results: Entropy 2.5-10x (base64_small=10x), Regex 1.5-8x (KeywordDetector_miss=8x, PrivateKeyDetector=6-8x), Scan file 1.4-2.2x, Baseline merge/trim 13-37x. scan_repo comparison invalid: Python create() is lazy (generators), Rust scans eagerly. Per-call targets >10x limited by PyO3 FFI overhead (~1-2us/call). Native Rust CLI would achieve full speedup without FFI bridge cost.
